bundle_parser.identified_role: parses the login-result-0x3F continuation bundle after the normal connection packet cipher stage
key_observations: Reads client +0x160 bytes into the shared network scratch buffer at connection +0x2C0 and then calls 0x14016BBF0 before extracting the five NUL-terminated strings.
The auxiliary object passed into 0x14016BBF0 comes from connection +0x2B8, matching the per-connection packet-cipher state already used for normal inbound packets.
This means the 0x3F continuation bundle is not delivered as plain post-login text. It is still decoded through the existing packet stream before the strings are consumed.

packet_decode_helper.identified_role: bytewise packet decode helper driven by a per-connection cipher/state table
key_observations: Advances through the mutable buffer one byte at a time and subtracts a pseudo-random byte derived from the caller-supplied state object.
Indexes dword state at object +0x04 onward using the current counter, which is consistent with a rolling packet-stream decode stage rather than string normalization.
Its use from 0x140159810 explains why the continuation fields can be parsed with ordinary zero-terminated string helpers immediately afterward.
Initializes or clears string-like fields at +0x10, +0x30, +0x48, and +0x60.
Initializes a nested subobject at +0x98 through 0x140299B50.
Resets the dword flag/counter at +0x128 to zero.

global_snapshot_helper.identified_role: copies six string-like values into process-global auth cache slots
key_observations: Builds a temporary six-string structure and then copies each field into a global block rooted at 0x140E58B08.
Uses repeated string-copy style helpers rather than any socket, HTTP, or job submission primitive.
This identifies the helper family as local object/cache management rather than the transport layer itself.

struct_copy_helper.identified_role: copy/swap helper for the repeated string-chunk structures used by the auth cache
key_observations: Moves multiple adjacent string-like chunks between temporary and global storage.
Appears underneath 0x140299C20 and related setup code, reinforcing the cache-snapshot interpretation.

global_slots.observed_role: cached scope-like slot consumed by shield/oauth/token
cached redirTo-like slot consumed by shield/oauth/token
OAuth service base/root used when composing shield/oauth/* endpoints
separate service base/root used when composing game-session/v1/tokens
dynamic token-bearing request slot reused by check_token and revoke-style builders
dynamic refresh-token-like request slot reused by shield/oauth/token and related builders
global request object pointer replaced by the OAuth/game-session builder family

field_name_pointer_table.notes: A second pointer slot resolves to the same refresh_token literal and is used by the later token-refresh builder.

token_builder.identified_role: builds a shield/oauth/token refresh-style request from cached auth state
key_observations: Allocates or replaces the global request object at 0x140E58A78.
Builds HTTP-like header entries including Authorization, Content-Type, and Accept.
Composes the endpoint from the OAuth base/root at 0x140E58B68 plus the literal shield/oauth/token.
Consumes dynamic values from the cached refresh-token-like slot at 0x140E58AB8, the cached scope-like slot at 0x140E58B38, and the cached redirTo-like slot at 0x140E58B50.
Uses the grant_type field name together with a fixed refresh_token mode, making this builder look like the concrete refresh-style OAuth exchange rather than the earlier raw continuation bundle.

check_token_builder.identified_role: builds a shield/oauth/check_token request using the cached token-bearing slot
key_observations: Replaces the same global request object at 0x140E58A78.
Composes the endpoint from the OAuth base/root at 0x140E58B68 plus the literal shield/oauth/check_token.
Pairs the token field name with the dynamic slot at 0x140E58AA0.

revoke_and_game_session_family.identified_role: neighboring builders that cover token revocation and a later game-session token stage
key_observations: One path composes shield/oauth/revoke from the OAuth base/root at 0x140E58B68 and uses token_type_hint together with token-bearing dynamic state.
A nearby later path composes game-session/v1/tokens from the distinct base/root at 0x140E58B80.
The game-session path also references the accountId literal, indicating a second service layer after the core OAuth step.

relationship_to_earlier_0x3f_artifact.summary: The earlier 0x14029BAB0 mapping still stands for the first continuation-bundle field quartet grant_type/token/redirTo/sig. The new 0x14029A0D0, 0x14029A3D0, and 0x14029AA20 family sits later or alongside that stage and operates on cached globals to build concrete shield/oauth and game-session requests.
relationship_to_earlier_0x3f_artifact.preserved_conclusion: The login-result-0x3F branch should now be read as a multi-stage desktop-auth pipeline: packet-cipher-decoded continuation bundle, local cache snapshot, and then concrete shield/oauth plus game-session request builders.

shared_consumer.identified_role: persists or updates a display/profile-like string at client +0xB70

supporting_observations: The unresolved fifth continuation string from 0x140159810 is passed to 0x1401303A0.
The later JX_DISPLAY_NAME consumer path in 0x14015CC20 also feeds its fetched value through 0x1401303A0.
Only a small number of callsites feed this sink, so the fifth continuation string is now likely part of the same display/profile-name semantic family.
fifth_string_inference.notes: This remains an inference rather than a proven final field name.

next_targets: Name the first and second cached global slots at 0x140E58B08 and 0x140E58B20 so the continuation bundle can be mapped cleanly into the later cached-request stage.
Prove whether the fifth continuation string and the later JX_DISPLAY_NAME value are identical fields or only converge on the same sink at 0x1401303A0.
Separate the exact function boundaries in the 0x14029AA20..0x14029AExx region so revoke and game-session token builders can be named independently.
Trace where the OAuth base/root at 0x140E58B68 and the game-session base/root at 0x140E58B80 are populated.
